name: Modal
what_it_does: Serverless cloud compute platform for running Python functions at scale with GPU support. Enables ML inference, training, data pipelines, and arbitrary Python workloads on-demand without managing infrastructure.
use_cases: Serverless GPU inference for custom ML models without managing GPU servers
Burst processing for AI workloads that need compute on demand
Running data pipelines and batch jobs with auto-scaling GPU workers
Hosting custom ML model inference APIs with automatic scaling
Fine-tuning and training ML models on ephemeral GPU clusters
not_for: Always-on latency-sensitive services (Modal has cold start overhead)
Non-Python workloads (Modal is Python-first with no REST API)
Simple CPU-bound tasks where cheaper compute suffices
best_when: You need serverless GPU compute for Python ML workloads without the DevOps overhead of managing CUDA environments, Docker, or Kubernetes.
avoid_when: You need always-on low-latency inference, non-Python runtimes, or predictable fixed costs.

auth.notes: Modal token (ID + secret) for CLI and SDK authentication. Tokens set via `modal token set` or MODAL_TOKEN_ID/MODAL_TOKEN_SECRET environment variables. No REST API — interaction is exclusively via the Python SDK.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $30/month free compute credits on free tier.
pricing.paid_tiers: GPU compute: A100 ~$0.59/hr; H100 ~$0.97/hr; CPU $0.0000016/second. Per-second billing.
pricing.notes: Per-second billing for actual compute time. Free tier includes $30 monthly credits — sufficient for development and moderate workloads. No minimum commitment.

performance.rate_limits: No explicit rate limits; queued execution based on available GPU capacity

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Python exceptions with clear messages. Container build failures show detailed logs. Runtime errors include full stack traces. Modal logs are accessible via CLI and dashboard for debugging.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Function calls are stateless and not inherently idempotent. Applications must manage idempotency at the application level. No built-in deduplication mechanism.
known_agent_gotchas: Cold start latency: first invocation after idle period requires container spin-up (seconds to minutes depending on image size)
Modal functions must be decorated with @app.function — standard Python functions cannot be called remotely without decoration
GPU container images take time to build on first deployment — pre-build and cache container images for faster startup
Python-only SDK — no REST API for access from non-Python agents or polyglot systems
Persistent volumes required for model weights that should not be re-downloaded per invocation
Secrets must be created in Modal workspace — local environment variables are not automatically available in remote execution
Modal apps must be deployed before .remote() calls work — deployment step required in agent setup

score_rationale: Modal is an excellent platform for Python-native ML workflows with outstanding documentation and generous free tier. fq_score reflects the clean Python SDK, good error messages, and reasonable pricing. af_score is penalized significantly by the Python-only SDK with no REST API (severely limiting cross-language agent access), cold start variability, and no idempotency. Agents that are themselves Python-based and need custom GPU inference get much more value here than the score suggests for non-Python contexts.
